---
title: "Recovery Results"
author: "Felix Pichardo"
date: "2025-02-28"
output: 
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    theme: cosmo
    highlight: tango
    code_folding: hide
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(tidyverse)
library(kableExtra)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
```

```{r}
# Function to read recovery data files
load_recovery_data <- function(file_path) {
  readRDS(file_path)
}

# Paths to data files
PROJ_DIR <- here::here()
REC_SIM_DIR <- file.path(PROJ_DIR, "Data", "sim", "recovery")

# List of files to process
recovery_files <- list.files(REC_SIM_DIR, pattern = "*.rds", full.names = TRUE)

# Load all recovery objects
recovery_data <- list()
for (file in recovery_files) {
  model_name <- basename(file) %>% 
    str_remove("recovery_igt_mod_batch_001_") %>%
    str_remove("_desc-sim_params.rds")
  
  recovery_data[[model_name]] <- load_recovery_data(file)
}
```


# Overall Recovery Performance
```{r}
# Create a function to extract overall metrics
extract_overall <- function(recovery_obj, model_name) {
  overall <- recovery_obj$overall
  overall$model <- model_name
  return(overall)
}

# Combine all overall metrics
overall_df <- bind_rows(
  lapply(names(recovery_data), function(name) {
    extract_overall(recovery_data[[name]], name)
  })
)

# Reorder rows for better comparison
model_order <- c("ddm", "ev", "pvldecay", "pvldelta", 
                "ev_ddm", "pvldecay_ddm", "pvldelta_ddm")

overall_df <- overall_df %>%
  mutate(model = factor(model, levels = model_order)) %>%
  arrange(model) %>%
  select(model, metric, value)

# Create a wider format for easier comparison
overall_wide <- overall_df %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = metric, values_from = value) %>%
  rename(Model = model, Correlation = correlation, RMSE = rmse, 
         Bias = bias, `Relative Bias` = relative_bias)

# Add model type grouping
overall_wide <- overall_wide %>%
  mutate(Type = case_when(
    Model == "ddm" ~ "DDM Only",
    Model %in% c("ev", "pvldecay", "pvldelta") ~ "RL Only",
    TRUE ~ "Hybrid (RL+DDM)"
  )) %>%
  select(Type, everything())

# Display nice table
kable(overall_wide, digits = 3, caption = "Overall Recovery Performance Metrics") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), 
                full_width = FALSE) %>%
  pack_rows("DDM Only", 1, 1) %>%
  pack_rows("RL Only", 2, 4) %>%
  pack_rows("Hybrid (RL+DDM)", 5, 7)
```

# Parameter-Specific Recovery
```{r}
# Function to create parameter tables for each model
create_param_table <- function(model_name) {
  param_df <- recovery_data[[model_name]]$by_parameter

  kable(param_df, digits = 3, 
        caption = paste("Parameter Recovery for", model_name)) %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), 
                  full_width = FALSE)
}
```

## DDM Only
```{r}
create_param_table("ddm")
```

## RL Models
```{r}
create_param_table("ev")
```

```{r}
create_param_table("pvldecay")
```

```{r}
create_param_table("pvldelta")
```

## Hybrid Models (RL+DDM)
```{r}
create_param_table("ev_ddm")
```

```{r}
create_param_table("pvldecay_ddm")
```

```{r}
create_param_table("pvldelta_ddm")
```

# Correlation Comparison by Parameter Type
```{r}
# Extract parameter-specific data
extract_params <- function(recovery_obj, model_name) {
  params <- recovery_obj$by_parameter
  params$model <- model_name
  return(params)
}

# Combine all parameter data
params_df <- bind_rows(
  lapply(names(recovery_data), function(name) {
    extract_params(recovery_data[[name]], name)
  })
)

# Add parameter type
params_df <- params_df %>%
  mutate(param_type = case_when(
    parameter %in% c("boundary", "tau", "beta") ~ "DDM Process",
    parameter %in% c("drift", "drift_con", "con") ~ "Consistency/Drift",
    parameter %in% c("wgt_pun", "wgt_rew", "gain", "loss") ~ "Valuation",
    parameter %in% c("update", "retend") ~ "Learning Rate",
    TRUE ~ "Other"
  ))

# Create grouped model type
params_df <- params_df %>%
  mutate(model_type = case_when(
    model == "ddm" ~ "DDM Only",
    model %in% c("ev", "pvldecay", "pvldelta") ~ "RL Only",
    TRUE ~ "Hybrid (RL+DDM)"
  ))

# Plot correlation by parameter type
ggplot(params_df, aes(x = parameter, y = correlation, fill = model_type)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  facet_wrap(~ param_type, scales = "free_x") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Parameter Recovery (Correlation) by Parameter Type",
       y = "Correlation",
       x = "Parameter",
       fill = "Model Type") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        legend.position = "bottom")
```

# RMSE Comparison by Parameter Type
```{r}
ggplot(params_df, aes(x = parameter, y = rmse, fill = model_type)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  facet_wrap(~ param_type, scales = "free_x") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Parameter Recovery (RMSE) by Parameter Type",
       y = "RMSE",
       x = "Parameter",
       fill = "Model Type") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        legend.position = "bottom")
```

```{r}
# Improved parameter recovery visualization
# This code replaces the section with issues in the original document

# Create a cleaner parameter type classification
params_df <- bind_rows(
  lapply(names(recovery_data), function(name) {
    extract_params(recovery_data[[name]], name)
  })
) %>%
  mutate(param_type = case_when(
    parameter %in% c("boundary", "tau", "beta") ~ "DDM Process",
    parameter %in% c("drift", "drift_con", "con") ~ "Consistency/Drift",
    parameter %in% c("wgt_pun", "wgt_rew", "gain", "loss") ~ "Valuation",
    parameter %in% c("update", "retend") ~ "Learning Rate",
    TRUE ~ "Other"
  )) %>%
  # Create more readable model names
  mutate(
    model_display = case_when(
      model == "ddm" ~ "DDM Only",
      model == "ev" ~ "EV Only",
      model == "pvldecay" ~ "PVLdecay Only",
      model == "pvldelta" ~ "PVLdelta Only",
      model == "ev_ddm" ~ "EV+DDM",
      model == "pvldecay_ddm" ~ "PVLdecay+DDM",
      model == "pvldelta_ddm" ~ "PVLdelta+DDM"
    ),
    # Create model family grouping
    model_family = case_when(
      model %in% c("ddm", "ev", "ev_ddm") ~ "EV Models",
      model %in% c("ddm", "pvldecay", "pvldecay_ddm") ~ "PVLdecay Models",
      model %in% c("ddm", "pvldelta", "pvldelta_ddm") ~ "PVLdelta Models"
    )
  )

# Define custom color palettes - distinct for each model family
model_colors <- c(
  "DDM Only" = "#4575b4",
  "EV Only" = "#74add1", 
  "PVLdecay Only" = "#74add1",
  "PVLdelta Only" = "#74add1",
  "EV+DDM" = "#f46d43",
  "PVLdecay+DDM" = "#f46d43", 
  "PVLdelta+DDM" = "#f46d43"
)

# Create a better plotting function that handles both metrics
plot_recovery_metrics <- function(data, metric = "correlation", title = NULL) {
  # Define y-axis label based on metric
  y_label <- if(metric == "correlation") {
    "Correlation (higher is better)"
  } else {
    "RMSE (lower is better)"
  }
  
  # Set plot limits and direction based on metric
  y_limits <- if(metric == "correlation") {
    c(0, 1)
  } else {
    c(0, max(data[[metric]], na.rm = TRUE) * 1.1)
  }
  
  # Create the plot with improved aesthetics
  p <- ggplot(data, aes(x = parameter, y = .data[[metric]], fill = model_display)) +
    geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +
    facet_wrap(~ param_type, scales = "free_x", ncol = 2) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = model_colors) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = y_limits) +
    labs(
      title = title,
      y = y_label,
      x = "Parameter",
      fill = "Model"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      strip.text = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      panel.spacing = unit(1, "lines"),
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray95", color = NA)
    )
  
  return(p)
}

# Function to create comparison plots for each model family
create_model_family_plots <- function() {
  # Create data subsets for each model family
  ev_data <- params_df %>% filter(model %in% c("ddm", "ev", "ev_ddm"))
  pvldecay_data <- params_df %>% filter(model %in% c("ddm", "pvldecay", "pvldecay_ddm"))
  pvldelta_data <- params_df %>% filter(model %in% c("ddm", "pvldelta", "pvldelta_ddm"))
  
  # Create correlation plots
  p1 <- plot_recovery_metrics(
    ev_data, 
    "correlation", 
    "Parameter Recovery (Correlation) for EV Model Family"
  )
  
  p2 <- plot_recovery_metrics(
    pvldecay_data, 
    "correlation", 
    "Parameter Recovery (Correlation) for PVLdecay Model Family"
  )
  
  p3 <- plot_recovery_metrics(
    pvldelta_data, 
    "correlation", 
    "Parameter Recovery (Correlation) for PVLdelta Model Family"
  )
  
  # Create RMSE plots
  p4 <- plot_recovery_metrics(
    ev_data, 
    "rmse", 
    "Parameter Recovery (RMSE) for EV Model Family"
  )
  
  p5 <- plot_recovery_metrics(
    pvldecay_data, 
    "rmse", 
    "Parameter Recovery (RMSE) for PVLdecay Model Family"
  )
  
  p6 <- plot_recovery_metrics(
    pvldelta_data, 
    "rmse", 
    "Parameter Recovery (RMSE) for PVLdelta Model Family"
  )
  
  # Return all plots
  return(list(
    ev_corr = p1,
    pvldecay_corr = p2,
    pvldelta_corr = p3,
    ev_rmse = p4,
    pvldecay_rmse = p5,
    pvldelta_rmse = p6
  ))
}

# Generate all the plots
model_family_plots <- create_model_family_plots()

# Display correlation plots one at a time for clarity
model_family_plots$ev_corr
model_family_plots$pvldecay_corr
model_family_plots$pvldelta_corr

# Display RMSE plots one at a time
model_family_plots$ev_rmse
model_family_plots$pvldecay_rmse
model_family_plots$pvldelta_rmse

# Create an alternative combined view comparing all models by parameter type
# This creates a different visualization that might be clearer for some comparisons

# Function to create parameter-focused plots
plot_by_parameter_type <- function(param_type_focus) {
  # Filter data for this parameter type
  param_data <- params_df %>% 
    filter(param_type == param_type_focus)
  
  # Create a cleaner model family grouping for side-by-side plots
  param_data <- param_data %>%
    mutate(
      model_group = case_when(
        model == "ddm" ~ "DDM Only",
        model %in% c("ev", "pvldecay", "pvldelta") ~ "RL Only",
        TRUE ~ "Hybrid (RL+DDM)"
      ),
      model_specific = case_when(
        model == "ev" | model == "ev_ddm" ~ "EV",
        model == "pvldecay" | model == "pvldecay_ddm" ~ "PVLdecay",
        model == "pvldelta" | model == "pvldelta_ddm" ~ "PVLdelta",
        TRUE ~ "DDM"
      )
    )
  
  # Plot correlation
  p_corr <- ggplot(param_data, aes(x = model_specific, y = correlation, fill = model_group)) +
    geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +
    facet_wrap(~ parameter, scales = "free_x", ncol = 3) +
    scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Parameter Recovery (Correlation) for", param_type_focus, "Parameters"),
      y = "Correlation (higher is better)",
      x = "Model Family",
      fill = "Model Type"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      strip.text = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      panel.spacing = unit(1, "lines"),
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray95", color = NA)
    )
  
  # Plot RMSE
  p_rmse <- ggplot(param_data, aes(x = model_specific, y = rmse, fill = model_group)) +
    geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +
    facet_wrap(~ parameter, scales = "free_y", ncol = 3) +
    scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
    labs(
      title = paste("Parameter Recovery (RMSE) for", param_type_focus, "Parameters"),
      y = "RMSE (lower is better)",
      x = "Model Family",
      fill = "Model Type"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      strip.text = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      panel.spacing = unit(1, "lines"),
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray95", color = NA)
    )
  
  return(list(correlation = p_corr, rmse = p_rmse))
}

# Create plots for each parameter type
ddm_process_plots <- plot_by_parameter_type("DDM Process")
consistency_plots <- plot_by_parameter_type("Consistency/Drift")
valuation_plots <- plot_by_parameter_type("Valuation")
learning_plots <- plot_by_parameter_type("Learning Rate")

# Display parameter-focused plots
ddm_process_plots$correlation
ddm_process_plots$rmse
consistency_plots$correlation
consistency_plots$rmse
valuation_plots$correlation
valuation_plots$rmse
learning_plots$correlation
learning_plots$rmse

# Create a single comprehensive visualization for quick overview
# Combined plot showing the best-performing models for each parameter

# Create summary dataset
param_summary <- params_df %>%
  group_by(parameter, param_type) %>%
  # For each parameter, find which model performs best
  summarize(
    best_corr_model = model_display[which.max(correlation)],
    best_corr_value = max(correlation),
    best_rmse_model = model_display[which.min(rmse)],
    best_rmse_value = min(rmse),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Plot best models by parameter
ggplot(param_summary, aes(x = parameter, y = best_corr_value, fill = best_corr_model)) +
  geom_col() +
  facet_wrap(~ param_type, scales = "free_x", ncol = 2) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set3") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) +
  labs(
    title = "Best Performing Models by Parameter (Correlation)",
    y = "Maximum Correlation",
    x = "Parameter",
    fill = "Best Model"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold")
  )
```
